segment-text: Toy's House
Current Draft 9/9/09
EXT. JOE'S BACKYARD - DAY
Hammer strikes nail. crooked. It hits off-center, driving the nail in Reveal the carpenter: JOE TOY (14), Our Hero. A scrawny, skater-type kid with greasy brown hair parted in the center.
EXT. JOE'S BACKYARD - DAY

On a magazine photo of an extreme biker taking off from a wooden ramp. The biker, and the ramp, look awesome. Joe lowers the magazine to reveal: His ramp. Not awesome. Low quality, warped wood. Odd angles. Nailheads protrude dangerously from different points across the surface. A valiant effort, but no. Joe grabs his TOOLBOX and walks inside.
INT. TOTTENVILLE HIGH SCHOOL, CLASSROOM - DAY
Joe sits in a middle row, doodling. The science teacher, DR. FERRARA (40s) fires questions at the class.
In any ecosystem we have indigenous flora and...? Robert? Shale?
KID (ROBERT)
No, not shale...I like where you're head's at though...

Ferrara hears Joe doodling in the otherwise silent class. Joe doesn't notice Ferrara approaching. We see the drawing: Joe, on horseback, riding into the FOREST. A girl rides with him, clutching him around the waist. He colors her dress in with a YELLOW HIGHLIGHTER. Ferrara STOMPS HIS FOOT once loudly next to Joe, snapping him out of his artistic trance. Joe Toy.
Flora and?
(turning page) Fauna.
Very good. An allamanda cathartica would be an example of which? (off Joe's silence) And an allamanda cathartica would be an example of which?
I've never heard those words before in my life.
That was my hope. No drawing in class, please. Allamanda cathartica. Allamanda cathartica. (noticing a raised hand) Kelly.
We land on KELLY (15), a pretty girl...in a YELLOW DRESS.
When Ferrara turns his back, Joe and Kelly make eye contact. Joe rolls his eyes at her ass-kissing. She sticks her tongue out. They're clearly buddies.
EXT. TOTTENVILLE HIGH SCHOOL - 2 P.M.
School's out. Students crowd the sidewalk. Joe walks his bicycle alongside him, keeping pace with Kelly.
What were you drawing in there? was obviously top secret.
It
Oh. Just some...blueprints. Patrick and me are working on this
bike ramp thing.
Blueprints? Huh. Funny?
That's funny.
You just don't strike me as like a "man's man," y'know? Construction and all. That's not an insult. Beat on Joe.
Smiling, but insulted.
How's Patrick doing, by the way?
He's fine. I think he's supposed to come back next week.
I was gonna say, if you guys want, you should come to Thirsty Thursdays tomorrow night.
"Thirsty Thursdays." idea what that is.
I have no
It's a keg. At Wolf's Pond. Paul's friends throw it every week once it starts getting warm out and
ohmygod-The boyfriend approaches, PAUL: 17, could be 36. Goatee, earring. He's bleeding noticeably from the shoulder.
What happened?? Hm?
Some dog bit me. I'm fine.
He kisses her 'hello' on the mouth.
What? For no reason??
Well I was hitting him and shit. (to Joe) What's up man.
Oh -- Paul, you know Joe. Bio together. We have
(handshake)
(inspecting the wound) Should we go to the hospital?
No. You want a bagel? want an egg bagel. Ugh! C'mon.
(to Joe) He always gets hurt, and he never
goes to the doctor. Joe raises his eyebrows in false fascination.
Starving, babe.
Okay, okay. See you tomorrow, Joe!
Joe waves, watching as Paul leads her away, shoving his hand into Kelly's back pocket as they walk. Off his misery:
EXT. TOWN OF HUGENOT - DAY

Joe rides his bike home from school. We get an impression of the town: a bank, a church, a barbershop. Quaint. EXT. WOODS - CONTINUOUS Joe steers through a narrow "trail" in the woods. with trees and brush. It's dense Craning up above the trees, we see that the woods are bordered on all sides by tracts of suburban houses. In the deep distance, factory pipes. Off the sound of a DOORBELL:
EXT. PATRICK'S HOUSE - DAY
Joe waits on the stoop. The door opens to reveal MRS. MCGUIRE, 42. Patrick's mom.
Patrick home? MRS. MCGUIRE Come in, Joseph.
INT. PATRICK'S HOUSE, GROUND FLOOR - CONTINUOUS
A modest country-theme home. Moments abound. Thomas Kinkade and Precious
(calling upstairs) Patrick! Joseph's here.
I'm coming down.
Stay where you are! He will come to you!
I'm fine, ma!

At the top of the stairs, PATRICK MCGUIRE comes into view. Though also 14, he's a brick shithouse, a man by any physical standard. There's an AIR CAST on his left foot. He hops down one step at a time.
Mother Cabrini, protect my son.
Stop praying, ma. Another few steps.
Look at you! You're sweating. You're sweating from the pain!
I'm sweating from you, ma. Two more stairs.
He's winded now.
I'm getting you a cold washcloth.
I don't want one.
Well it's happening. How about you, Joseph? Would you like a cold washcloth?
I would take one, yeah.
No one is getting a cold washcloth.
INT. PATRICK'S HOUSE, LIVING ROOM - LATER
On TV: "STREET FIGHTER 2" for Super Nintendo. The boys sit in spitting distance of the screen, concentrating.
My mom reminds me of Blanka.
Because she wears cutoff jeans?
No, because everything she says, in my head, sounds like Blanka's crazy victory roar. Listen.
On TV: After a knockout, BLANKA stands on his hind legs and roars three times.
That's the sound I hear whenever she speaks. Just the jibberish of an undisciplined animal.
You hear that all day? All day.
He pauses the game.
I have to get out of this house, man.
What do you mean?
I mean I can't stand another minute of it. Of them. They're down my throat all day. They don't stop.
Well, you'll be back at school Monday, right? You can tough it out.
No, you don't get it. They're killing me. Like, I'm getting hives.
No you're not.
Patrick lifts his shirt: a few small hives are scattered on his torso.
Yeah, shit is right.
INT. JOE'S HOUSE, KITCHEN - NIGHT
Family dinner. Joe sits with his father, FRANK (51), and his sister LISA (19), who wears a college-team hoodie.
Daddy, can Colin come tomorrow? really wants to meet you guys.
Can I get some sweet potato casserole? (then) Who's Colin?
He's my boyfriend of seven months.
Hm. What are his prospects? does he plan to support you?
He's Sri Lankan.
Right, right. Aren't we all. (passing food) Put that back over by you. Move the water.
Speaking of tomorrow -- can you drive me to Wolf's Pond at like 8?
Joe notices Frank staring at him in mild shock.
Tomorrow. Thursday night, Joe. What do you think we're sitting here talking about?
What are we talking about?
Is this a joke? Carol is coming over Thursday. We're having Game Night. I told you about this weeks ago.
You never told me about that!
That's horseshit, Joe. I told you at least twice, and I watched you write it on your forearm in permanent marker.
Joe glances at his forearm. There is some faded text.
Well what if I don't want to meet "Carol"? I don't make you meet all the girls I bang out. Lisa snickers.
This is not a debate, Joe! plans are made. (MORE)
Just be there, and be pleasant. She's making lamb stew for us. She's going out of her way.
Lamb stew? What is this, Beowulf? I'm not touching it.
Believe me, you're touching it.
It'll be fun, Joe. meet Colin. You'll get to
(to Lisa) I think I'm done hearing that name tonight, okay?
Joe simmers at Frank, livid.
FRANK (CONT'D)
Joe rises silently and moves to the wall phone.
What are you doing?
Joe picks up the receiver and shoots Frank a threatening look.
We're supposed to be past this, Joseph! For Christ's sake! Remember what we talked about. Think of people who really need to call 911!
A moment of tension. Then, Joe opens the dishwasher, shoves the phone in, and leaves.
(after him) I don't know who that's supposed to be hurting!
EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - DAY
Joe watches as his friend ANGELO, 14, sticks a large FIRECRACKER into a mailbox. Angelo turns to flee.
Come on come on come on! They run across the street and hide behind some shrubs.
nebbishy kid, AARON (also 14), waits for them there. They stare at the mailbox. Nothing happens.
Did it light? The mailbox EXPLODES. Ha!
Got you. / Remarkable.
An INDIAN GUY, 30s, comes out and immediately spots the boys.
We tired of this game yet?
Not till you learn English, dothead!
Huh?? I have a double masters from Georgetown. Your father cleans pools. Go home!
In the background, a storm door opens on one of the houses. A little old Italian lady pokes her head out. Angelo'S MOM. ANGELO'S MOM Angelo! Venire all'interno e spostare vestiti!
Essere di destra la!
She retreats inside.
Shit. I gotta go shift the wash. (to Indian guy) Fuck you, Mola Ram!! What?!
I'm out too. Thirsty Thursdays. Oh God.
You're going to that? Of course.
Everyone's going.
Not me. My goddam shit-eating father won't let me. (then) And Kelly invited me too.
So what, Kelly invited you. She's got a boyfriend. Who's twice your size.
Right. The cave troll. name again? Ju'tuun?
Paul. I can't believe that guy is seventeen years old. He looks like he works for Con Ed.
Yeah. (frustrated) Shit.
Well no big deal, man. There will be other...best times of your life.
INT. JOE'S HOUSE, LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
Game night. Lisa, Frank and Joe sit around a monopoly board, joined by COLIN, 19, bookish; and CAROL, 40, far too good for this situation. Joe sits slumped, miserable.
Carol, the lamb was delicious. Very tender. It felt like I was chewing avocado meat.
Thanks Colin. I boil it first.
I thought you might have. want to presume. I didn't
Colin, no one knows what you're talking about.
Carol ROLLS.
Marvin Gardens.
Pass.
Passing on Marvin Gardens?
Well look at this.
Ballsy. I'm broke.

You pissed it all away on purples. (going through her cards) What the hell is this, Virginia? If you're gonna rely on colors, go red, orange, yellow. Strong colors. They attract the eye. People want to land there. They
will themselves to.
Royal blue. Also a fierce color.
Colin, again, we're unclear as to what you're saying.
Joe sits up.
Don't listen to this clown. His whole game is railroads. He's got blinders on to the world.
Who's "he"? I'm right here. Blinders.
People need transportation, Joe. They need to get from A to B.
Joe points his hands out from his temples: Blinders.
Just roll, Frank.
He does.
Lisa, do you remember when you bought B&O and wouldn't trade him, and he quit?
I didn't "quit." I just wasn't having fun anymore. Why would I play if I'm not having fun? What's the point of a game? People play games to heighten life, to forget themselves.
That's beautiful.
Shut the fuck up, Colin.
(laughing) Is that true, Frank? You actually quit Monopoly? How old are you?
Frank tenses up, uncomfortable.
Oh yeah, he always quits. He once quit because Lisa hit free parking six times.
Any sane person would have walked away from that game. Something wasn't right and you goddam know it.
Carol laughs, and Joe joins her. Frank shoots Joe a look. Joe shoots one back, relishing the moment of comeuppance. Frank moves his gamepiece.
I will absolutely buy that... (counting out money) Hello Colin. Hello.
Do you want to be my friend?
Very badly, sir.
Joe watches this exchange, not trusting it.
Okay then. Right now -- Tennessee and St. James. I'll give you Park Place and Indiana.
Whoa whoa whoa whoa-FRANK Let the men talk, Joseph. On Joe: fuming. Yes.
Clearly, yes I would do that.
Frank, that makes no sense. kill us all.
There will be collateral damage, yes. But war is hell.
Joe looks at the board -- he sees what's happening. His piece is in jail. Frank's building on orange. It's a setup.
(to himself) He's pricing me out...
What's that?
(big smile) Your roll, Joey Joe-Joe.
This is bullshit, Dad! You're pricing me out of the goddam game!
(eyeing board) Oh man...he is.
Oh come on Frank.
He's your son.
You can't do that Dad! collusion.
I can do whatever I want, Joe. house, my rules.
Frank lights a cigar and leans back. A suburban Bugsy. He smiles at Joe, rubbing it in. Joe's lip quivers, but he tries not to break, tries not to give Frank the satisfaction.
Let's just have a fun game.
No?

Hey, I'm not the one who started telling fun little, funny little stories. He's just mad because he doesn't want to be here, so he's being a shit. (then) Roll the die, Joe. Let's see what happens. If you need a loan, maybe we can work something out at a fair interest rate.
A tense, miserable beat of silence.
for the kitchen.
Then Joe rises, heads
That was a cruel thing to do, Frank.
Is he quitting?
INT. JOE'S KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
Joe, red-faced, picks up the wall phone. INSERT - A switchboard operator picks up.
911, what is your emergency?
BACK IN JOE'S HOUSE: Frank knows what's happening.
Ah shit -- he's doing it!
Frank darts at Joe, but Joe runs into the bathroom and locks the door.
INT. JOE'S BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
Joe sits on the toilet, covering his ear.
(into phone) I want to report a theft...yes ...up to one thousand dollars in cash and assets...
Hang it up Joe!
(into phone) 28 Rye Avenue. No, he's actually in the house right now...
Loud BANGING as Frank attempts to break the door down. moves toward the corner of the room.
(into phone) His name is Frank Toy, brown hair...height unknown...He's obsessed with railroads...
The door SPLINTERS.
INT. JOE'S ROOM - NIGHT

Through Joe's window, we see Frank talking to two police officers. He holds the Monopoly board and makes desperate, animated gestures. The officers nod. Move back to reveal Joe, watching, holding a flashlight. room is dark. He shines the flashlight in short spurts out the window. dot-dot, dash-dash-dash, dot-dot-dot... After a few beats, we hear THUMPING from outside. climbing stairs. Dot- Feet Frank opens the door.
Stop signaling.
Joe shines the flashlight beam into Franks's eyes. squints and shuts the door.

Joe lies on the bed, miserable. He spins something around between his thumb and forefinger. Flashlight on: a small green MONOPOLY HOUSE. He looks at it, then past it, and freezes...something caught his eye. The wall across from him: Though we notice an "Excalibur" poster and other assorted nerd items, Joe's focus is on the huge SHADOW being cast by the monopoly house. Joe regards this "house" shape... Then shines the flashlight across the room, illuminating the TOOLBOX sitting atop his dresser. Beat on Joe, thinking...
INT. SPANISH CLASS - DAY

Patrick enters on his boot, carrying a team duffel bag. The teacher conducts the class to recite the words on the board: ENTIRE CLASS Recepcion detras, Patrick! They applaud. Patrick smiles through the awkwardness.
INT. SPANISH CLASS - LATER
Patrick recites translations, sitting by the window. TEACHER'S VOICE ?Que color es perros?
(with the class)
Algunos perros son marron, algunos

The sound of KNOCKING breaks his concentration -- Joe's outside, at the window. Behind him, a scrawny, olive-skinned kid sits on a bicycle: BIAGGIO, 14, quiet but dedicated. Joe makes a "come here" motion. Patrick turns his palms upward in a gesture of powerlessness. EXT. SPANISH CLASS WINDOW - CONTINUOUS
(mouthing the words) Come outside for a second.
A stern Patrick points to his desk, then points to the front of the classroom.
(mouthing) Come on. One second.
Patrick writes something on loose leaf and presses it against the window. It says "I am in school right now"
INT. SPANISH CLASS - CONTINUOUS

Joe and Biaggio sit on the window ledge. Patrick sees from the corner of his eye, then shakes his head. Joe takes a sandwich out of his bag, prepared to wait it out. TEACHER'S VOICE Patrick, ?Como viejo es tu perro?
(caught off guard) Um, no tengo un perro, profesor.
Who are those boys outside? Does anyone know those boys? Are they your friends, Dudley?
A black kid behind Patrick picks his head up.
Patrick's been talking to them for five minutes, man! Why do I get blamed?
Because of what you are!
EXT. TOTTENVILLE HIGH SCHOOL, COURTYARD - AFTERNOON
Patrick walks across the street with Joe.
Don't do that shit anymore. gonna get me in trouble. You're
I would get detention, not you. You did nothing wrong.
A car full of students drives by. A kid hanging out the window hollers "McGuire!" Patrick absently waves.
How do you never get caught? about the truant officer?
Yeah, the truant officer. He's fucking 19. I'll shit in his mouth. Cutaway to a baby faced Cop-in-a-Box idling on the curb.
(hopping on his bike) Get on.
EXT. WOODS - AFTERNOON
Biaggio hacks through brush with a MACHETE. walk the dirt path behind him.
Come on man. I can't walk all day like this.
We're almost there. yards, about.
Almost where? What's happening right now??
Joe and Patrick
EXT. WOODS, CLEARING - MOMENTS LATER
The come to a large, treeless area. No civilization in sight. Joe walks to the epicenter, the about-faces and stretched his arms, like "welcome."
Do you want your room facing
sunrise, or the sunset?
What are you talking about?
Our new house. No, "home." We're building a home on this very spot.
What, like a tree-house?
No, like a living-house -- I'm moving out. And I want you to come with me.
Biaggio thrusts a shovel toward Patrick.
Who is this kid? (to Biaggio) Who the fuck are you?
It's Biaggio.
student?
What are you, an exchange
He's been in our class since the 4th grade. (beat) We'll get to him later man - are you in? Patrick rubs his eyes.
Joe isn't kidding.
You're serious about this.

Of course I'm serious. Look, you can't live with your parents, I certainly can't live with Shit-Ass Frank any more. If we do this we can make our own rules, make our own choices! Live and die by our wits, like men! What do we need? A few 2x10s and a foundation?
It's not that simple, Joe.
It is, man. With my mind, and your hands, we can pull this off. We're preternaturally skilled craftsmen.
Joe, we almost built a ramp, and I got a 92 in Shop. That doesn't mean we can build a house!
Come on man. Break ground with me. You're my best friend.
Biaggio looks at Joe, crestfallen.
(to Biaggio) Don't take that to heart Biaggio. Who knows what the future holds. (to Patrick) Well?
Patrick takes a deep breath.
Look, I won't tell anyone what you're doing, I promise. But this is insane. You know that, right?
Joe's last resort:
It's this or the hives, man. Silence.
I'll see you later.
Patrick boots off into the forest.
INT. PATRICK'S KITCHEN TABLE - NIGHT
Patrick jams him palm into a Heinz bottle, drizzling ketchup on a burger. Mom and Dad sit on either side. MRS. MCGUIRE
(to Patrick) What did Coach Locke say when he saw you?
Nothing. He just said he was glad to have me back.
I saw your sub play last Thursday. "Moscato," they called him. Yes.
That's his last name.
I didn't like him. He had a weak chin. (looking at Patrick's plate) You do that often? You eat the hamburger, then the fries? You don't mix it up?
I don't know. Sometimes.
Patrick, have you heard about this Die Hard movie? Die Hard 5? It's set in outer space. Huh?
Mmm...It's not a Die Hard movie. think it's called Festival something.
Yes, that's the one -- Festival. Bruce Die Hard is in it, though. Are you gonna go see Festival with your friends? MR. MCGUIRE
"Festival."
Are you talking about "The Fifth Element"??
Right! The Elements. It looks very impressive. Tell us how it is.
Did I say I was seeing it? no interest in it.
I have
Yes, it's with Bruce Element, and
the other one, who's the bad guy? He's been in a lot, he's very good. "Samuel Clemens."
His name is not Samuel Clemens.
Well the actor, not the character.
(rubs his eyes) I know what you mean. Samuel
Clemens is Mark Twain's pen name. The actor is Gary Oldman.
Mm...no, that doesn't sound right.
Well maybe you guys should go see it! Treat yourselves. Get out of the house for a night.
Maybe we will.
(re: Patrick's food) The flavors compliment each other, if you wanted to do some of one, then some of the other.
(agitated) I know, Dad.
Your Aunt Janette is coming Friday afternoon, Patrick. I told her you'll be around. I'm going to put some cashews out.
I don't have to call her 'aunt' anymore, right? I'm fourteen. She's of no blood relation. You went to camp with her.
She loves you like an aunt, she
showers you with gifts.
She loves you, Patrick.
She gets me weird things that I don't want. She got me a woodburning kit.
I know she did. And now it just sits there. Too much goes to waste in this world.
Patrick just shakes his head. He bites into his burger. McGuire can't stop staring at Patrick's plate.
Is that a usual thing for you, though? (to wife) He eats all of one food, and then
moves onto all of another food.
Come on. That's how the blind eat. We didn't raise you to do that. To be blind.
(losing it) But how does it affect you?? comment on it??
Out of nowhere, Mom starts WHISTLING.
Can you whistle? Can you whistle, Patrick? I have to breath in to do it.
No no no. You're supposed to breath out. Let the diaphragm support it.
They both whistle out of key with one another. Patrick loses some color in his face and starts breathing erratically.
INT. JOE'S BEDROOM/PATRICK'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
The phone RINGS. Joe answers right away.
Hello?
Cut to Patrick's (intercut between rooms as needed). He's standing in front of a full length mirror, covered in HIVES. I'm in.
Huh?
I'm in, I said. The house. do it.
(grinning) Fuckin...yes! Awesome man...okay, we break ground tomorrow at 5AM. Good shit!
Eleven's fine.
INT. KELLY'S ROOM - THE NEXT MORNING
Kelly sits on her bed listening to a CD. door, then a Mom-type opens it. A KNOCK on her
Kel -- Vicki's coming up. Okay.
The best friend, VICKI (15) enters. Dark hair, dark soul.
You're not gonna believe this.
She hands Kelly a magazine.
'Architectural Digest.'
So?
Turn to page 23.
Kelly turns. We see a feature on JOE, standing before his pristine new House in the Woods. Oh my God!
Joe Toy??
He built that house with his bare hands.
Wait -- he built this??
Well, Patrick and Biaggio helped with parts and labor, but the whole thing was Joe's baby. God, a guy who can work with his hands... that's the ultimate.
(reading) It's got a game room... and a botanical garden...and a lazy river! Ugh.
He must be rich.
Rich, or just brilliant. crafty as hell.
Tight on Kelly, lost in the article.
I can't believe a man like this has been under my nose for a whole year. We hear more KNOCKING.
It seems to come from far away... SOUNDBRIDGE TO:
INT. JOE'S SHOWER - REAL LIFE, 10AM
The knocking snaps Joe out of his DAYDREAM. His hair is sculpted into a shampoo mohawk. He's showering.
Joe! You've been in there for fifty-five minutes!
INT. JOE'S HOUSE, UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER
Frank waits outside the door, messy and unshaven. turns off. He knocks again. The water
INT. JOE'S BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
Joe brushes his teeth. the door.
He shoots a contemptuous glance at
I have a tee time.
INT. JOE'S HOUSE, UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
Frank knocks again.
You have to talk to me eventually, Joe.
Silence. After a beat, Joe runs out, naked and still soaked. His hands are cupped over his genitals.
(averting his eyes) Jesus.
As Joe scampers into his room:
You've gotta bring underwear in with you, Joe! You're 14 years old! It's not cute anymore!
EXT. JOE'S NEIGHBORHOOD - DAY

Bird's eye of Joe bursting out of his side gate on his bike. We move up and over the rooftops, heading past subdivisions, strip malls, until the woods appear in the distance. We move in until treetops fill the frame. We linger on the dense green tableau. It could be anywhere in the world.
EXT. WOODS, SITE OF HOUSE - MUCH LATER
Patrick, Joe and Biaggio stand in a large rectangular hole in the earth, DIGGING. Judging by the stains and general demeanor, the fun part is over. We hear grunts, wheezing.
(to Biaggio) Did you talk to your father about concrete?
He says it's impossible, and he says if I ask again he's going to throw my binder in the garbage.
(stops shoveling) Well shit man, where does that leave us?
Relax, we'll think of a substitute. We could use sod, we could use clay, we--
This isn't the fucking French Open, Joe! We need a concrete foundation. That's like rule one.
We could use dirt.
Pack it hard.
You want to fill this hole with dirt?
Biaggio shrugs.
Patrick throws his shovel and climbs out of
Where are you going? Relax man! This is a flawed process.
(climbing) No shit. Call me when you have some idea what's going on.
C'mon, keep your eye on the prize here! We need you, Tum Tum!
And we're not using nicknames from "Three Ninjas," either. Patrick is gone. him.
Biaggio starts after him, but Joe stops
Let him go, Colt.
INT. CONVENIENCE STORE - LATER
Joe walks in, utterly filthy. The door chimes. Guy behind
Not cool, guys. I just had the floors buffed yesterday.
By the refrigerators, Patrick, also very filthy, browses soft drinks. Joe approaches.
How can I make you happy here?
(hands Joe a Snapple) First, you're gonna buy me a Guava Mania. Second, I want you to make a fucking plan! Read a book!
Hey man, I never said it would be easy, I just said it would be worth it. Good things come to those who wait. Friends are the best therapy.
I've been to your house, asshole! Those are your refrigerator
magnets. Patrick moves up an aisle. Joe follows.
Fine. I'll do some research, okay? We both will.
Patrick hands Joe a box of frozen waffles.

These are coming too. (back on subject) I've done the research, Joe! Did you know that pine cones are a legitimate source of fiber? That the red baneberry is edible, but the white one is deadly? That skunk spray can blind you? No, you didn't.

(sigh) Yes, fine, you know things. But no one learns as much from books as they do from actual real life, okay? You can bring a fucking meat thermometer and your Cub Scout manual if you want, but at some point we're gonna have to dick up and trust our instincts..
Yo! Out of the store! Not gonna say it again!
(ignoring attendant) Joe, all I'm saying is, let's do this right. (MORE)
I'm not in the mood for another Joe Toy disaster.
Fine, yes, agreed. That's all I'm saying too. (beat) So you're still on board?
Look at me. I'm on board.
Joe exhales, relieved.
These too. Wow.
Patrick throws a bag of pork rinds on
You're a class act.
MUSIC CUE: "WHITE WINTER HYMNAL" by FLEET FOXES

Joe plucks books from LIBRARY SHELVES. He stacks them on the checkout counter: "WALDEN." "105 OUTDOOR RECIPES." "SO, YOU WANT TO BUILD A CABIN." Blueprints unroll. Reveal Joe, Patrick and Biaggio reviewing them in an empty classroom. It feels like a war room. In the woods, Joe and Biaggio dump piles of timber next to Patrick, who ties them together with small vines. They are a symphony of sweat and grime. Joe and Biaggio watch as Patrick confidently bites into an herb. There's an open reference book in Patrick's hand. Joe stacks more books at the library counter: "SELF RELIANCE AND OTHER ESSAYS." Then, the following comic books: "SPAWN," "BATMAN VS. PREDATOR," "SUPERMAN: CAMELOT FALLS." Then another reference book, "I'D RATHER BE HUNTING." Patrick messes around with some dry twigs and flint, trying to get a spark. Once he does, he blows on it - FIRE. On a suburban street, Biaggio halts his bike, seeing something. Swoosh pan to a sofa left on the curb as trash. Biaggio and Joe set the sofa on a lumpy wood floor -- the house in the woods. One wall has been erected.
